medical_history.chief_complaint: Excessive bleeding from the oral mucosa following mild trauma
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Diagnosed with type 3 VWD at the age of 14 months. Treated with VWF/FVIII concentrate Haemate P for posttraumatic intracranial haemorrhage at 15 months. Received multiple treatments for mucosal bleeding, trauma, and ankle bleeding over the next 3 years. Developed possible allergic reaction (urticaria and dizziness) after 31 exposure days of Haemate P. Later treated with Wilate for a minor diagnostic procedure without side effects. Developed fever and a soft mass on her neck, leading to surgery. Post-surgery, undetectable levels of VWF activity and low FVIII:C were found, leading to suspicion of inhibitors against VWF. Treated with rFVIIa and high doses of rFVIII.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Type 3 von Willebrand disease diagnosed at 14 months. Multiple treatments with VWF/FVIII concentrates for various bleeding episodes.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Very high levels of anti-VWF antibodies (>3 SDs above the mean result of samples from 10 healthy individuals)
Homozygous deletion of a C nucleotide in exon 18 of the VWD gene (NM_000552.3:c.2435delC) causing a frameshift mutation and premature termination codon (p.Pro812ArgfsTer31)
